Figure 7. Visualization of the experimental results for the super-resolution (4×) task with a contamination factor of ρ = 0.10 and a Gaussian noise level of σ = 0.05.

Figure 1. Overview of our proposed Robust-GD and Robust-CG methods. At each timestep ti, we first calculate the signal estimate x̂0. Next, we refine the measurement y to ȳ using explicit noise estimation. We then formulate a robust objective function designed to handle outliers and approximately solve the corresponding optimization problem using either the gradient descent method (Robust-GD) or the conjugate gradient method (Robust-CG). After obtaining the result x̄0, we sample x̃ti−1 ∼ N (αti−1 x̄0, σ 2 tiIn). After N iterations, we use x̃t0 as the final reconstructed image.

Figure 6. Visualization of the relationship between the distortion metric PSNR and computational cost for the CelebA Gaussian deblurring task. The computational cost is measured in terms of (left) average inference time over 100 images, (middle) number of function evaluations, and (right) number of forward operator evaluations.
